name: User Management & Self-Service Onboarding
description: Comprehensive user management architecture that eliminates manual AUTHORIZED_USERS environment variable pattern, enables self-service tenant registration and user onboarding, integrates with existing CRM pipeline (Twenty CRM via n8n), and provides admin interfaces for user/tenant management. Critical foundation for March 2026 pilot customer onboarding and revenue generation.

businessJustification.problem: Current AUTHORIZED_USERS env var pattern requires manual SSH access for every new user, blocks self-service signup, causes E2E test failures, and prevents automated customer journey from waitlist → trial → paying customer
businessJustification.impact: Blocks March 2026 Canadian pilot customer onboarding, prevents revenue generation, creates operational bottleneck for user provisioning
businessJustification.opportunity: Enable $5K-$10K MRR by Sept 2026 through automated customer acquisition and tenant provisioning
businessJustification.roi: Eliminates 100% of manual user provisioning overhead, reduces onboarding time from days to minutes, enables pre-seed raise milestone (June 2026)

before.userProvisioning: Manual SSH access to production servers to update env vars and restart services
before.tenantCreation: Manual database operations via Prisma Studio or SQL scripts
before.teamInvitations: Email-based manual account creation by platform admin
before.customerJourney: Waitlist → manual outreach → manual provisioning → manual access grant
before.crmIntegration: One-way: Waitlist → n8n → Twenty CRM (no reverse sync)
before.scalability: Limited by manual operations, high operational cost per tenant
before.compliance: No audit trail for user provisioning, manual PIPEDA data handling

after.authorization: Database-driven UserAuthorizationService with Redis caching, automatic session validation
after.userProvisioning: Self-service registration with email verification, automated tenant provisioning
after.tenantCreation: TenantProvisioningService automatically creates tenant + schema + admin user + demo data
after.teamInvitations: Secure token-based invitation system with 7-day expiration, role assignment
after.customerJourney: Waitlist → email verified → self-service signup → automated trial → upgrade to paid
after.crmIntegration: Bidirectional: Registration updates CRM, CRM webhooks trigger provisioning
after.scalability: Zero manual intervention, automated tenant lifecycle, unlimited growth capacity
after.compliance: Full audit trail, automated PIPEDA compliance, tenant-isolated data retention

architectureDecisions.approach: Database-Driven User Management with Multi-Tenant Integration
keyPrinciples: Build on completed multi-tenant foundation (MT-M1 through MT-M5)
Leverage existing OAuth authentication (Google OAuth via Passport.js)
Replace environment variable authorization with database + Redis cache
Automate tenant provisioning during registration workflow
Maintain existing CRM integration (Twenty CRM via n8n) and extend bidirectionally
Admin UI for zero-SSH user/tenant management
Invitation-based team member onboarding with secure tokens
Progressive disclosure: Basic registration → Trial features → Paid subscription

alternatives.name: Third-party auth service (Auth0, Clerk)
alternatives.rejected: High recurring cost ($200-500/month), vendor lock-in, existing OAuth investment

alternatives.name: Keep AUTHORIZED_USERS pattern with automation
alternatives.rejected: Still requires env var management, no audit trail, doesn't solve E2E testing issues

alternatives.name: Manual onboarding with CRM-only tracking
alternatives.rejected: Doesn't scale, blocks self-service model, high operational cost

rationale: Extends existing multi-tenant foundation without architectural rework
Provides strong audit trail for PIPEDA/SOX compliance
Zero additional licensing costs beyond existing infrastructure

securityModel.authorization: Database-driven UserAuthorizationService with role-based access control (RBAC)
securityModel.sessionManagement: Redis-backed sessions with automatic regeneration after privilege escalation
securityModel.invitationSecurity: Cryptographically secure tokens (32+ random bytes), 7-day expiration, single-use
securityModel.tenantIsolation: Row-Level Security (RLS) with app.current_tenant_id session variable (from MT-M3)
securityModel.auditTrail: All user/tenant operations logged in audit_logs table with tenant_id isolation
securityModel.passwordPolicy: bcrypt hashing (cost factor 12) for optional password auth, minimum 10 characters
securityModel.apiSecurity: JWT tokens with tenant claims, rate limiting (100 req/15min per user)

integration: Uses existing Tenant model (tenant_id, subscription_tier, status)
Relies on RLS policies (app.current_tenant_id) for data security
Extends User model with registration fields (email_verified_at, last_login_at)
Integrates with existing StakeholderRole enum (admin, owner, contractor)

implementation: TenantProvisioningService executes workflow: Create tenant → Generate schema → Migrate → Seed → Create admin
UserRegistrationService handles waitlist → email verification → tenant creation lifecycle
Atomic transactions with full rollback on provisioning failure
Automated data retention policies per tenant (GDPR/PIPEDA compliance)

enhancement: Bidirectional sync: Registration → Twenty CRM (update lead status to 'trial_user')
CRM webhook handler: Twenty CRM trial_to_paid trigger → Subscription upgrade
Lifecycle stages: waitlist → email_verified → tenant_created → trial → paid_customer → churned

additions: User CRUD: List, create, edit, deactivate users (with tenant filtering)
Tenant CRUD: List, create, edit, suspend, delete tenants
Role assignment: Grant/revoke roles per user per tenant
Invitation management: Create, resend, revoke team invitations
User impersonation: Platform admins can impersonate tenant users for support

milestones.name: Database Models & Migrations
milestones.description: Create Prisma schema extensions for user registration, invitations, tenant members, and subscription history
deliverables: prisma/schema.prisma - 4 new models (UserInvitation, UserRegistration, TenantMember, SubscriptionHistory)
Updated User model with registration fields (email_verified, email_verified_at, last_login_at, registration_completed)
Updated Tenant model with trial tracking (trial_started_at, trial_ends_at, converted_at)
completionCriteria: UserInvitation model with token, email, tenant_id, role, status, expires_at, invited_by
UserRegistration model tracking lifecycle: waitlist → verified → tenant_created → trial → paid
TenantMember junction table for many-to-many user-tenant-role assignments
SubscriptionHistory for audit trail of tier changes (trial → basic → professional → enterprise)
All foreign keys with proper constraints and cascading rules
Indexes for performance: tenant_id, email, token, status, expires_at
Migration tested against staging database with rollback plan

schemas: UserInvitation: 10 fields, unique token index, expires_at index
UserRegistration: 12 fields tracking CRM integration lifecycle
TenantMember: 6 fields with composite unique constraint (user_id, tenant_id)
SubscriptionHistory: 8 fields for SOX compliance audit trail
migrations: Forward migration: CREATE TABLE statements with constraints
Rollback migration: DROP TABLE statements for clean reversal

milestones.name: Backend Services Layer
milestones.description: Implement core service classes for registration, invitations, tenant provisioning, authorization, and CRM integration
deliverables: Unit tests for all 5 services (>80% coverage)
completionCriteria: UserRegistrationService: register(), verifyEmail(), completeProfile() methods
UserInvitationService: createInvitation(), acceptInvitation(), revokeInvitation() with token generation
TenantProvisioningService: provisionTenant() executes atomic workflow (tenant + admin + schema + seed)
UserAuthorizationService: isAuthorized() with Redis caching (5-minute TTL), replacing env var checks
CRMIntegrationService: syncToTwentyCRM() bidirectional with lifecycle stage updates
All services use TenantContextService for RLS isolation
Error handling with specific error types (RegistrationError, InvitationExpiredError, ProvisioningError)
Transaction-safe operations with automatic rollback on failure

evidence.serviceClasses: 5 services, average 300+ lines each, full TypeScript types
evidence.unitTests: 25+ test cases covering happy paths, error cases, edge cases
evidence.redisIntegration: Authorization cache with automatic invalidation on user/role changes

milestones.name: Registration & Invitation API Endpoints
milestones.description: Create RESTful API endpoints for user registration, email verification, and team invitation workflows
completionCriteria: POST /api/registration/signup - Create registration from waitlist email
POST /api/registration/verify-email - Verify email with token
POST /api/registration/complete-profile - Complete registration with company/role info
GET /api/registration/status/:email - Check registration status
POST /api/invitations - Create team invitation (authenticated, admin role required)
POST /api/invitations/:token/accept - Accept invitation and create user account
GET /api/invitations/pending - List pending invitations for current user
DELETE /api/invitations/:id - Revoke invitation (admin only)
All endpoints validated with Zod schemas for request/response
Rate limiting: 5 req/hour for signup, 100 req/15min for authenticated endpoints
Email notifications: Welcome email, invitation email, verification email

evidence.endpoints: 11 RESTful endpoints with full CRUD operations
evidence.validation: Zod schemas for all inputs, type-safe responses

milestones.name: Admin UI for User & Tenant Management
milestones.description: Build admin dashboard pages for managing users, tenants, invitations, and roles without SSH access
deliverables: apps/web/src/app/admin/users/page.tsx - User management table with filters
apps/web/src/app/admin/tenants/page.tsx - Tenant management with status controls
completionCriteria: User table: Search, filter by tenant/role/status, edit, deactivate, impersonate
Tenant table: Search, filter by tier/status, edit, suspend, view metrics (user count, project count)
Invitation page: Create invitation with email/role/tenant, resend, revoke, view status
Role assignment: Multi-select roles per user per tenant (admin, owner, contractor)
Impersonation: Platform admin can impersonate tenant user (logged in audit_logs)
Responsive design: Works on desktop and tablet (admin on mobile not required)
Permissions: Only is_platform_admin=true users can access admin UI
Real-time updates: Auto-refresh tables when changes occur (WebSocket or polling)

evidence.pages: 3 admin pages with full CRUD operations
evidence.permissions: Middleware blocking non-admin access, tested with E2E

milestones.name: CRM Integration & Customer Lifecycle
milestones.description: Extend CRM integration to bidirectionally sync user lifecycle stages and enable automated customer journey
deliverables: n8n workflow updates for bidirectional sync
Analytics integration for conversion funnel tracking
completionCriteria: Lifecycle stages: waitlist → email_verified → tenant_created → trial → paid_customer → churned
Webhook: POST /api/webhooks/crm/trial-to-paid - Upgrade user from trial to paid subscription
Webhook: POST /api/webhooks/crm/lead-qualified - Trigger automated registration email
n8n workflow: Sync registration → Twenty CRM with lifecycle stage update
n8n workflow: Twenty CRM opportunity_won → Subscription tier upgrade
Analytics events: registration_started, email_verified, tenant_created, trial_activated, subscription_paid
Conversion funnel metrics: Waitlist → Trial conversion rate, Trial → Paid conversion rate
Email automation: Trial expiring (7 days before), trial expired, upgrade prompts

evidence.webhooks: 3 webhook endpoints with HMAC signature validation
evidence.n8nWorkflows: 2 updated workflows with error handling and retry logic
evidence.emailAutomation: 3 automated email sequences based on lifecycle stage

milestones.name: AUTHORIZED_USERS Migration & Cleanup
milestones.description: Migrate existing AUTHORIZED_USERS environment variable to database, update OAuth routes, deploy to production
deliverables: E2E tests validating new authorization flow
completionCriteria: Migration script: Parse AUTHORIZED_USERS, create User records with email_verified=true
OAuth routes: Replace authorizedUsers.includes(email) with UserAuthorizationService.isAuthorized()
Redis cache: Warm cache with existing users before cutover
Blue-green deployment: New code deployed, old env var still works during overlap
Validation: All existing users can still authenticate post-migration
Cleanup: AUTHORIZED_USERS env var removed from all environments (dev, staging, production)
Documentation: Update deployment docs, remove references to AUTHORIZED_USERS pattern
Monitoring: Track authorization failures, cache hit rate, latency (p95 < 50ms)

evidence.migration: Dry-run and production execution logs with user counts
evidence.deployment: Zero downtime during cutover, no authentication failures
evidence.cleanup: AUTHORIZED_USERS removed from .env, docker-compose, GitHub secrets
evidence.monitoring: Grafana dashboard showing authorization metrics (cache hit rate >95%)

external: Email service (SendGrid, AWS SES, or similar) - to be provisioned
Domain verification for email sending (DKIM, SPF, DMARC) - to be configured

value.estimatedSavings: $50K-$100K annual operational cost reduction (eliminates manual provisioning overhead)
value.roi: 500%+ ROI within 6 months (implementation cost ~$20K vs. $100K+ revenue enabled)
value.revenueEnabled: $5K-$10K MRR by Sept 2026 through automated customer acquisition
value.operationalImpact: Zero manual intervention required for user/tenant provisioning post-implementation

complianceImpact.pipeda: Automated data retention policies per tenant, full audit trail for user provisioning
complianceImpact.gdpr: Self-service data export, right to erasure, consent management during registration
complianceImpact.sox: 7-year audit log retention for user/tenant changes, immutable subscription history
complianceImpact.cmmc: Enhanced access control with RBAC, MFA support (future), all authentication logged

pilotBlocking.risk: March 2026 Canadian pilot delayed due to manual onboarding bottleneck
pilotBlocking.mitigation: M1-M3 completion by Feb 15 enables basic registration, sufficient for pilot

dataMigration.mitigation: Blue-green deployment with overlap period, dry-run migration, comprehensive E2E testing

emailDeliverability.risk: Verification emails marked as spam, blocking registration flow
emailDeliverability.mitigation: Proper DKIM/SPF/DMARC setup, use reputable email service (SendGrid/AWS SES), test deliverability

crmIntegration.risk: Twenty CRM webhook failures cause registration failures
crmIntegration.mitigation: CRM sync is async/non-blocking, retry logic with exponential backoff, fallback to manual sync

technical: Zero manual SSH access required for user/tenant provisioning
100% of new users onboard via self-service registration
E2E test success rate 100% on staging with OAuth + database auth
business: March 2026 pilot customer onboarded via self-service (no manual provisioning)
Pre-seed raise milestone achieved (June 2026) enabled by revenue traction
operational: Zero support tickets for 'cannot login' issues (replaced with self-service password reset)
Platform admin time on user provisioning reduced from 2 hours/week to 0
Tenant creation time: Days → Seconds (automated provisioning)
CRM data quality: 100% registration → CRM sync (currently ~80% manual entry)

phase1.name: Internal Testing (Dev + Staging)
phase1.scope: M1-M4 completed, tested on staging environment
phase1.validation: E2E tests passing, admin UI functional, registration flow works for test users

phase2.name: Pilot Customer Onboarding
phase2.scope: M5-M6 completed, deployed to production, AUTHORIZED_USERS migrated
phase2.validation: March pilot customer self-service registration successful, CRM integration working

phase3.name: Public Waitlist Conversion
phase3.scope: All existing waitlist users (~50-100) invited to register via automated campaign
phase3.validation: Waitlist → Trial conversion rate >25%, no manual provisioning needed

phase4.name: Revenue Generation & Optimization
phase4.scope: Trial → Paid conversion optimization, subscription tier upgrades, feature gating
phase4.validation: $5K-$10K MRR achieved, 5-10 paying customers, pre-seed raise completed

metrics: Registration funnel: Started → Email verified → Profile completed → Tenant created
Authorization performance: Cache hit rate, p50/p95/p99 latency, failure rate
Invitation metrics: Created, accepted, expired, revoked per tenant
Tenant provisioning: Success rate, failure reasons, rollback frequency, duration (p95)
CRM sync: Success rate, latency, retry count, manual intervention needed

dashboards.grafana: User management dashboard with real-time registration metrics
dashboards.twentyCRM: Lead lifecycle dashboard with stage progression

registrationFailures.action: Alert ops team, investigate email service or database issues

authorizationCacheMiss.action: Investigate Redis performance or cache invalidation logic

crmSyncFailures.action: Alert integration team, check n8n workflow and Twenty CRM API health

mfaSupport.description: Multi-factor authentication via TOTP (Google Authenticator) for enhanced security

ssoIntegration.description: Enterprise SSO via SAML 2.0 / OpenID Connect for large customers

advancedRbac.description: Custom role definitions, permission granularity at feature level

userImpersonation.description: Enhanced impersonation with session recording for support/debugging

selfServiceBilling.description: Stripe integration for subscription management, invoicing, payment methods
